evals.prompt: What's the best approach to rate-limiting an API? Give me your best answer.
evals.expected_output: A well-researched answer recommending one approach with a runner-up alternative, supported by adversarial stress-testing across multiple candidates. Output must NOT be DEGRADED (real sub-agents, not self-play).

assertions.text: Output is not marked DEGRADED — execution used real sub-agents, not inline self-play
Process log confirms Phase 2 critique agent and 3 author agents were actually spawned
Process log confirms 3 Condorcet pairwise comparison sub-agents were actually spawned
Output contains both a recommended solution AND a distinct runner-up alternative
Evidence of 3 divergent candidates generated in Phase 1 (A/B/C labeling or equivalent)
Output reflects domain research — references established rate-limiting patterns (e.g., token bucket, sliding window, leaky bucket, fixed window) rather than only generic advice
Winner recommendation includes concrete implementation guidance beyond just naming a pattern
